title: physostegia correllii
intro: Physostegia correllii, also known as the Texas snakeroot, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and providing relief from menstrual cramps. Physostegia correllii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various ailments.
